causal_question: Among U.S. House districts with very close elections around the 50% Democratic vote threshold in the 78th Congress, what is the causal effect of electing a Democrat (versus a Republican) on the probability that the representative signs a civil-rights discharge petition?
vague_question: In very close 1943-44 House races, how much did electing a Democrat rather than a Republican change whether the member signed civil-rights discharge petitions?
